mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.15% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2021-06-15 indicates sustained accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.15%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month employment growth measured by PAYEMS above 200 thousand signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145,759 thousand as of 2021-06-15 reflects post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances when PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 200 thousand, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicates strengthening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 620,214 million and HOUST at 1,572 thousand as of 2021-05-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST show month-over-month growth, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 99.5 signals supply-side weakness that reduces near-term earnings growth expectations and increases equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 99.5 or declines > 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.0951 as of 2021-06-15 indicates near-trend production
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% year-over-year combined with unchanged federal funds rate signals monetary policy lag that increases real rate expectations and equity duration risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged for 2+ months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 270.981 and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2021-06-15
regime_hint: monetary policy lag during inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances when inflation accelerates above 2.5% with unchanged policy rates, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 6.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.0% or increases > 0.3 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.9% as of 2021-06-15 indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances when UNRATE rises above 6.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized rate signals weakening economic momentum that compresses equity valuations and earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% or declines > 1.5 percentage points quarter-over-quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,086.375 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2021-01-15
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration or early recession warning
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,400 thousand units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) growth slowing below 0.2% month-over-month indicates synchronized demand weakness that signals recession risk and equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,400 thousand AND RSAFS monthly growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,572 thousand and RSAFS at 620,214 million as of 2021-05-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction or early recession signal
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 53% of instances when both HOUST < 1,400 thousand and RSAFS monthly growth < 0.2%, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
